title: palicourea axillaris
intro: Palicourea axillaris, also known as the "Cinchona tree" or "Red Quinine tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is a rich source of quinine, a natural antimalarial agent, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Palicourea axillaris include its ability to treat malaria, as well as its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Palicourea axillaris plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
